sectiondef.header: Memory controller status register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Memory controller interface 0 interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interface 1 interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interface 0 interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 1 interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 0 raw interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 1 raw interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 0 ECC interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interface 1 ECC interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interface 0 ECC interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 1 ECC interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 0 raw ECC interrupt status mask.
Memory controller interface 1 raw ECC interrupt status mask.

sectiondef.header: Memory interface configurartion register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Memory controller interface 0 chip select mask.
Memory controller interface 0 data width mask.
Memory controller interface 1 chip select mask.
Memory controller interface 1 data width mask.
Memory controller interface exclusive masks mask.

sectiondef.header: Set configuration register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Memory controller interfce0 interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interfce1 interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller low power state mask.
Memory controller interfce0 ECC interrupt enable mask.
Memory controller interfce1 ECC interrupt enable mask.

sectiondef.header: Clear configuration register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Memory controller interface 0 interrupt disable mask.
Memory controller interface 1 interrupt disable mask.
Memory controller low power exit mask.
Memory controller interface0 interrupt clear mask.
Memory controller interface1 interrupt clear mask.
Memory controller interface0 ECC interrupt disable mask.
Memory controller interface1 ECC interrupt disable mask.

sectiondef.header: Clear configuration register bit definitions and masks and shift
Set cycles register bit definitions and masks and shift
Set opmode register bit definitions and masks

briefdescription.para: Set opmode set memory width mask.
Set opmode set burst align mask.
Set opmode memory width value for 8-bit flash.
Set opmode memory width value for 16-bit flash.
Set opmode memory width value for 32-bit flash.

sectiondef.header: Refresh period register bit definitions and masks
Opmode register bit definitions and masks
User status register bit definitions and masks
User config register bit definitions and masks
ECC status register bit definitions and masks
ECC mem config register bit definitions and masks and shifts

briefdescription.para: ECC cfg page size value for 512 byte page.
ECC cfg page size value for 1024 byte page.
ECC cfg page size value for 2048 byte page.
This enum defines the onfi commands.

detaileddescription.para: The user is required to allocate a variable of this type for every flash device in the system. A pointer to a variable of this type is then passed to the driver API functions.

briefdescription.para: Ecc position for 16 bytes spare area.
Ecc position for 32 bytes spare area.
Ecc position for 64 bytes spare area.

detaileddescription.para: ONFI commands.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each System Monitor/ADC device in the system.
This structure defines the onfi command format sent to the flash.

detaileddescription.para: ONFI commands.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each System Monitor/ADC device in the system.
This function initializes the HW ECC block based on flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the flash is not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the software variables related to ECC generation, ECC checking and writing ECC bytes in spare bytes.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the flash is not supported.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a specific page from NAND device using HW ECC block.

detaileddescription.para: It checks for the ECC errors and corrects single bit errors. The multiple bit error are reported as failure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is a pointer to the destination buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function reads a specific page from NAND device.

detaileddescription.para: This doesn't use the HW ECC block for checking ECC errors.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is a pointer to the destination buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function writes a specific page in the NAND device using HW ECC block.

detaileddescription.para: The ECC code is written into the spare bytes of the page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is a pointer to the source buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function writes a specific page in the NAND device.

detaileddescription.para: This doesn't use the HW ECC block for ECC code generation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is a pointer to the source buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the ECC value from the ECC registers.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer to fill the ECC value.

briefdescription.para: This function corrects the ECC errors.

parameterdescription.para: is the buffer which holds the data read from the page.
is the calculated ECC value.
is the ECC read from the spare area.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the ECC error is corrected.
XST_FAILURE if the ECC error is not corrected.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the page data from the AXI Data Phase Address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer pointer to store the byte.
is the number of bytes to read.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the data to the AXI Data Phase Address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer pointer to write the data from.
is the number of bytes to write.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether SMC controller busy in processing a request.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

simplesect.para:
- TRUE if SMC is busy
FALSE if SMC is free

briefdescription.para: This function sends a NAND command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to XNandPs structure
is the NAND command to send
is the page offset required for specific commands
the column offset required for specific commands

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the HW ECC block based on flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the value of ECC config to update.

briefdescription.para: This function writes ECC MEM CMD1 register with EccCmd value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is register value to write.

briefdescription.para: This function writes ECC MEM CMD2 register with EccCmd value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is register value to write.
is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the Bad Block Table(BBT) descriptors with a predefined pattern for searching Bad Block Table(BBT) in flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the Bad Block Table(BBT) if present in flash.

detaileddescription.para: If not it scans the flash for detecting factory marked bad blocks and creates a bad block table and write the Bad Block Table(BBT) into the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sends read status command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the NAND flash and gets the geometry information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a specific XNandPs device/instance.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the flash device to read or write any data.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
points to the XNandPs device configuration structure.
is the base address of SMC controller.
is the base address of NAND flash.

simplesect.para: The user needs to first call the XNandPs_LookupConfig() API which returns the Configuration structure pointer which is passed as a parameter to the XNandPs_CfgInitialize() API.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *DestPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_Read

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the Flash device and copies it into the specified user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: It doesn't check for the bad blocks while reading the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.
is the destination address to copy data to.
is the user buffer to which spare data must be copied.

simplesect.para: This function reads sequential pages from the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *DestPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_ReadCache

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from the Flash device using read page cache command and copies it into the specified user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: It doesn't check for the bad blocks while reading the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to read from.
is number of bytes to read.
is the destination address to copy data to.
is the user buffer to which spare data must be copied.

simplesect.para: This function reads sequential pages from the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *SrcPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_Write

briefdescription.para: This function programs the flash device(s) with data specified in the user buffer.

detaileddescription.para: The source and destination address must be aligned to the width of the flash's data bus. It doesn't check for the bad blocks while writing to the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to write to.
is number of bytes to write.
is the source address to write the data from.
is the user buffer which contains buffer to write into spare data area.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.

simplesect.para: This function writes number of sequential pages into the Flash device.

memberdef.argsstring: (XNandPs *InstancePtr, u64 Offset, u32 Length, void *SrcPtr, u8 *UserSparePtr)
memberdef.name: XNandPs_WriteCache

briefdescription.para: This function programs the flash device(s) with data specified in the user buffer using program cache command.

detaileddescription.para: The source and destination address must be aligned to the width of the flash's data bus. It doesn't check for the bad blocks while writing to the flash pages that cross block boundary. User must take care of handling bad blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the flash data address to write to.
is number of bytes to write.
is the source address to write the data from.
is the user buffer which contains buffer to write into spare data area.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.

simplesect.para: This function writes number of sequential pages into the Flash device.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the spare area of a page.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the page number from where spare data is read.
is pointer to the buffer where the spare data is filled.

briefdescription.para: This function write to the spare area of a page.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the page number to write.
is pointer to the buffer which holds the data.

briefdescription.para: This function erases a specific block in the NAND device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the block number of the device.

listitem.para: XST_NAND_WRITE_PROTECTED if the flash is write protected.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XNandPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xnandps.h for the definition of XNandPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

detaileddescription.para: This function searches the Bad Block Table(BBT) in flash and loads into the memory based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function searches the BBT in flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the BBT descriptor pattern to search.

briefdescription.para: This function scans the NAND flash for factory marked bad blocks and creates a RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function converts the Bad Block Table(BBT) read from the flash to the RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer which contains BBT read from flash.

briefdescription.para: This function writes Bad Block Table(BBT) from RAM to flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the BBT descriptor to be written to flash.
is the mirror BBT descriptor.

briefdescription.para: This function marks the block containing Bad Block Table as reserved and updates the BBT.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the BBT descriptor pointer.

briefdescription.para: This function updates the primary and mirror Bad Block Table(BBT) in the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether a block is bad or not.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the block number.

briefdescription.para: This function marks a block as bad in the RAM based Bad Block Table(BBT).

detaileddescription.para: It also updates the Bad Block Table(BBT) in the flash.

parameterdescription.para: is the pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the block number.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the data from flash.

detaileddescription.para: It is used for reading the control information from flash like ID and Parameter page.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer pointer to read the data.
is the length of data to read.

briefdescription.para: This function sends reset command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sends read ID command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sends read parameter page command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the CRC on the parameter page buffer.

detaileddescription.para: This is taken from the ONFI 1.0 specification.

parameterdescription.para: is the parameter page buffer.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the NAND flash parameter page defined by ONFI 1.0 specification.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is a buffer pointer to fill the data.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if parameter page read successfully.
XST_FAILURE if parameter page is not read successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function writes command data to flash.

detaileddescription.para: It is used for writing the control information like set features.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the buffer pointer to write the data.
is the length of data to write.

briefdescription.para: This function sends Get Feature command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the feature value to read.

briefdescription.para: This function sends Set Feature command to the flash device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XNandPs instance.
is the feature value to Set.

briefdescription.para: Max page size of NAND flash.
Max spare bytes of a NAND flash page.
Page is not valid in command phase.
Column is not valid in command phase.
AXI Data width for last transaction while reading and writing.
ECC error correction byte position mask, bits[11:3] of error code.
ECC error correction bit position mask, bits[0:2] of error code.
OneHot is used to check if one and only one bit is set.

detaileddescription.para: This Macro returns 1 if the value passed is OneHot.

briefdescription.para: Scan the second page for bad block information.
Page offset of Bad Block Table Desc.
Block shift value for a block in BBT.
Num of blocks in one BBT entry.
Bad block pattern offset in a page.
Bad block pattern offset in a large page.
Bad block pattern to search in a page.
Block shift mask for a Bad Block Table entry byte.
This macro returns the Block shift value corresponding to a Block.

parameterdescription.para: is the block number.

briefdescription.para: ONFI Change Read Column command (1st cycle)
ONFI Change Read Column command (2nd cycle)
ONFI Read cache enhanced command (1st cycle)
ONFI Read cache enhanced command (2nd cycle)
ONFI Block Erase interleaved command (2nd cycle)
ONFI Page Program interleaved command (2nd cycle)
ONFI Copyback program interleaved command (2nd cycle)
This file contains the implementation of the interface functions for XNandPs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the XNand driver's static initialization functionality.
This file implements ONFI specific commands which are used to get the parameter page information.
This module implements the ONFI specific commands.
This file contains identifiers and low-level macros/functions for the NAND Flash controller driver.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of NAND flash devices in the system.
This file implements the Bad Block Management(BBM) functionality.
This file implements the Bad Block Management (BBM) functionality.
Refer to the header file xnandps.h for more detailed information.
This module supports for NAND flash memory devices that conform to the "Open NAND Flash Interface" (ONFI) Specification. This modules implements basic flash operations like read, write and erase.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release 1.01a nm 28/02/2012 Fixed 16-bit issue with ONFI commands like read, write and read status command. The config structure width is updated after ONFI query with the parameter page width. 1.02a nm 20/09/2012 Removed setting of set_cycles and set_opmode register values as it is now done in FSBL using the PCW generated files. CR#678949. 1.03a nm 10/22/2012 Fixed CR# 673348. 1.04a nm 04/15/2013 Fixed CR# 704401. Removed warnings when compiled with -Wall and -Wextra option in bsp. 04/25/2013 Implemented PR# 699544. Added page cache read and program support. Added API's XNandPs_ReadCache and XNandPs_WriteCache for page cache support. Added ECC handling functions XNandPs_EccSetCfg, XNandPs_EccSetMemCmd1...etc, to support better usage of ECC block for page cache commands. Modified Read/Write API's so that there is common code for normal read/write and page cache commands. Disabling/Re-enabling ECC block in read/write API's of spare bytes since we don't calculate ECC for spare bytes. 2.01 kpc 07/24/2014 Fixed CR#808770. Update command register twice only if flash device requires >= four address cycles. 2.2 sb 01/31/2015 Use the address cycles defined in onfi parameter page than hardcoding this value to 5 for read and write operations. 2.8 akm 07/06/23 Update the driver to support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: See xnandps_bbm.h for more details.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release 1.03a nm 10/22/2012 Fixed CR# 683787. 2.4 nsk 06/20/2018 Fixed BBT offset overflow in XNandPs_WriteBbt() and XNandPs_ReadBbt(), overflow causes incorrect BBT writes. 2.9 sb 01/03/2024 Use FlashBbt array of nandps structure instead of local Buf array in XNandPs_WriteBbt() and XNandPs_ReadBbt(), to avoid stack overflow.

detaileddescription.para: This is similar to the Bad Block Management which is a part of the MTD subsystem in Linux. The factory marked bad blocks are scanned initially and a Bad Block Table(BBT) is created in the memory. This table is also written to the flash so that upon reboot, the BBT is read back from the flash and loaded into the memory instead of scanning every time. The Bad Block Table(BBT) is written into one of the the last four blocks in the flash memory. The last four blocks are marked as Reserved so that user can't erase/program those blocks.

There are two bad block tables, a primary table and a mirror table. The tables are versioned and incrementing version number is used to detect and recover from interrupted updates. Each table is stored in a separate block, beginning in the first page of that block. Only two blocks would be necessary in the absence of bad blocks within the last four; the range of four provides a little slack in case one or two of those blocks is bad. These blocks are marked as reserved and cannot be programmed by the user. A NAND Flash device with 3 or more factory bad blocks in the last 4 cannot be used. The bad block table signature is written into the spare data area of the pages containing bad block table so that upon rebooting the bad block table signature is searched and the bad block table is loaded into RAM. The signature is "Bbt0" for primary Bad Block Table and "1tbB" for Mirror Bad Block Table. The version offset follows the signature offset in the spare data area. The version number increments on every update to the bad block table and the version wraps at 0xff.

Each block in the Bad Block Table(BBT) is represented by 2 bits. The two bits are encoded as follows in RAM BBT. 0'b00 -> Good Block 0'b01 -> Block is bad due to wear 0'b10 -> Reserved block 0'b11 -> Factory marked bad block
While writing to the flash the two bits are encoded as follows. 0'b00 -> Factory marked bad block 0'b01 -> Reserved block 0'b10 -> Block is bad due to wear 0'b11 -> Good Block

The user can check for the validity of the block using the API XNandPs_IsBlockBad and take the action based on the return value. Also user can update the bad block table using XNandPs_MarkBlockBad API.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release

detaileddescription.para: See xnandps.h for more information about this driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release

detaileddescription.para: See xnandps.h for more information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release

detaileddescription.para: See xnandps_onfi.h for more information.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release 1.01a nm 28/02/2012 Added support for 8Gb On-Die ECC NAND flash parts (CR 648463). Fixed 16-bit issue with ONFI commands like read, write and read status command. 1.03a nm 10/22/2012 Fixed CR# 673348. 1.04a nm 04/25/2013 Implemented PR# 699544. Added page cache read and program commands to ONFI command list. Reading the cache features during read param page. 2.7 sg 03/18/21 Added validation check for parameter page.

detaileddescription.para:
The following commands are supported currently.
Reset
Read ID
READ Parameter Page
Read Status
Change Read Column
Get Features
Set Features

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release 1.04a nm 04/25/2013 Implemented PR# 699544. Added page cache read and program commands to ONFI command list.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a nm 12/10/2010 First release 2.8 akm 07/06/23 Update the driver to support for system device-tree flow. 2.10 akm 10/04/24 Retrieve the 'reg' property value from smcc node 'ranges' 2.11 vlt 12/14/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details.
